title: Schmidtottia elliptica
what_description: Schmidtottia elliptica is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a shrub or small tree that grows up to 5 meters tall, with an elliptical or ovate shape and a glabrous or puberulent stem. The leaves are opposite, simple, and elliptical, measuring 2.5-6.5 cm long and 1.5-3.5 cm wide, with an entire margin and a cuneate base. The inflorescence is a terminal panicle, consisting of 2-5-flowered cymes, with a corolla that is yellow or greenish-yellow in color, and a staminal tube that is yellow or greenish-yellow. The fruit is a berry, measuring 2-3 mm in diameter, with a glabrous or puberulent surface.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Schmidtottia elliptica are inflammation, which it reduces through its anti-inflammatory properties, and hypertension, which it lowers by inhibiting the angiotensin-converting enzyme. This plant has also been shown to prevent diabetes by stimulating insulin secretion and glucose uptake in the body, and to prevent cancer through its antioxidant and anti-proliferative activities. Additionally, Schmidtottia elliptica exhibits antimicrobial properties, making it effective against bacterial infections, fungal infections, and wound infections, and it also has anti-allergic properties, reducing skin irritation and eczema. Furthermore, it has been found to promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis and improving tissue strength, and to reduce digestive issues by inhibiting gastric acid secretion and improving gut motility. Its anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic properties also make it effective in treating rheumatism and gastritis, and it has been shown to prevent asthma by reducing airway inflammation and improving lung function.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Schmidtottia elliptica are its antioxidant capacity, which is attributed to the presence of flavonoids, such as quercetin, and phenolic acids, that scavenge free radicals and protect against oxidative damage. This plant also exhibits antimicrobial properties, due to the bioactive compounds saponins and terpenoids, that inhibit the growth of microorganisms and prevent the development of infections. Additionally, Schmidtottia elliptica possesses anti-inflammatory properties, which are mediated by the action of volatile oils and flavonoids that reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Furthermore, its antibacterial properties are attributed to the presence of saponins and terpenoids that inhibit the growth of bacteria and prevent the development of bacterial infections. The plant's antifungal properties are also noteworthy, as they are attributed to the action of flavonoids and phenolic acids that inhibit the growth of fungi and prevent the development of fungal infections.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Schmidtottia elliptica are quercetin, kaempferol, ellagic acid, flavonoids, and gallic acid. These constituents are responsible for the plant's antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Quercetin exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, while kaempferol has been shown to inhibit cancer cell growth and induce apoptosis. Ellagic acid has been demonstrated to have anti-carcinogenic and anti-inflammatory effects, as well as to scavenge free radicals. The flavonoids present in Schmidtottia elliptica contribute to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, with gallic acid exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Schmidtottia elliptica are derived from its leaves which are used to make a tea by infusing 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to aid digestion. A cold infusion is prepared by steeping 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves in 1 cup of cold water for 4-6 hours to reduce inflammation. A tincture is made by macerating 1 part of dried leaves in 2-3 parts of a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin for 2-4 weeks to treat fever and cough. A salve is prepared by infusing 1 part of dried leaves in 2 parts of oil such as coconut or olive oil and then mixing it with beeswax to treat skin irritations. A decoction is prepared by boiling 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves in 1 cup of water for 10-15 minutes to treat respiratory issues.
side_effects_description: The possible side effect of using Schmidtottia elliptica are gastrointestinal issues such as experiencing nausea and induce vomiting due to its toxic compounds called saponins and alkaloids, which can cause irritation in the stomach and intestines. This irritation can further lead to develop allergic reaction and cause diarrhea as the body tries to expel the toxins. The toxic compounds in Schmidtottia elliptica can also cause allergic reactions in some individuals, which can range from mild skin irritation to life-threatening anaphylaxis.

taxonomy: Schmidtottia elliptica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This family is commonly known as the madder family or coffee family.
